summary: Three primary specs for the pre-clarification questions are absent: runtime/gc.md (direct backing spec for #653 — Q1 is entirely about gc.rs), runtime/module.md (direct backing spec for #655 — Q2 is entirely about module.rs), and runtime/builtins.md (primary spec for #654 — Q4 explicitly cites builtins.rs:475). All three are only reachable via README.md line references, not listed as direct entries. More critically, stdlib/native-implementations.md is missing: it defines the exact module-registration, symbol-registration, and error-mapping pattern that ALL 7 new native stdlib modules (#652 atexit, #653 gc, #654 types, #655 importlib, #656 codecs, #657 errno, #666 tracemalloc) must follow. Finally, pattern-matching.md is irrelevant to this group — PEP 634 match/case implementation has no connection to any of the 7 in-scope issues; its cited justification references ast and inspect modules which belong to the stdlib-introspection group.

checklist_results.item: All affected crates/areas from pre-clarifications are covered by at least one spec
checklist_results.note: Q1 (GC infrastructure — gc.rs atomic RC + mark-sweep, gc.collect(), gc.disable()/enable(), threshold at 700 allocs): runtime/gc.md is the direct spec for gc.rs. It is NOT in the reference context table; only mentioned via README.md line 73. Q2 (Module registry — module.rs, mb_import(), circular protection, importlib.import_module() delegation): runtime/module.md is the direct spec for module.rs. Also absent; only mentioned via README.md line 87. Q3 (Memory tracking — gc.rs tracks containers, coarse tracemalloc stats): gc.rs is the backing infrastructure — gc.md covers R1 (container tracking) and R5 (threshold_allocs) which are the exact foundations for tracemalloc start/stop/get_traced_memory. runtime/gc.md absent. Q4 (Type objects — builtins.rs:475 type() builtin, need to synthesize FunctionType/GeneratorType/ModuleType class objects): runtime/builtins.md is the direct spec. Absent; only referenced via README.md line 76. Furthermore, stdlib/native-implementations.md — which defines how native stdlib modules are registered — is absent entirely despite all 7 issues requiring new native module files.

checklist_results.item: Relevance scores are reasonable (high = directly implements, medium = related, low = background)
checklist_results.note: README.md at MEDIUM: inflated. README.md is a pure index table with no R-prefixed requirements, no acceptance criteria, and no behavioral contracts. Its value is LOW (navigation only). The artifact uses it as a proxy to acknowledge that gc.md, module.md, and builtins.md are relevant — but then omits all three from the table. all-mamba-p0.md at MEDIUM: R1 (Module System) and R5 (Builtins Conformance) are marginally relevant as architectural context for #655 and #654, but they address multi-file compilation architecture and CPython conformance testing — neither directly implements the import machinery or type-object synthesis these issues require. LOW would be more accurate. pattern-matching.md at LOW: even LOW implies background relevance. PEP 634 pattern dispatch (R1-R8) has no connection to any of the 7 in-scope issues. The correct classification is NOT APPLICABLE — should be removed.

checklist_results.item: Key requirements listed per spec are accurate (match actual requirement IDs)
checklist_results.note: The line references and requirement IDs cited in the artifact are factually accurate where verifiable. README.md line 73 → runtime/gc.md confirmed. README.md line 87 → runtime/module.md confirmed. README.md line 76 → runtime/builtins.md confirmed. README.md lines 92-126 → stdlib module table confirmed (actual lines 91-127). all-mamba-p0.md R1 Module System & Imports at lines 20-23 confirmed. R5 Builtins Conformance at lines 40-42 confirmed. The problem is not wrong requirement IDs — it is that the most relevant specs are absent from the table entirely, making the listed requirements incomplete rather than incorrect.

pattern-matching.md is irrelevant. Its requirements cover PEP 634 structural match/case: R1 (parser pattern types — literal, capture, wildcard, sequence, mapping, class, OR, AS, guard), R2 (AS patterns), R3 (mapping **rest), R4 (case-branch type narrowing), R5 (HIR pattern nodes), R6 (AST→HIR lowering), R7 (nested pattern MIR), R8 (Cranelift codegen for dispatch). None of these address atexit hooks, GC collect/disable/enable, type object synthesis, importlib import machinery, codec registry, errno constants, or tracemalloc snapshots. The reference context's justification ('background context for ast and inspect modules') cites modules that are in the stdlib-introspection group, not this group.

issues.description: runtime/gc.md is absent. This is the primary spec for #653 (gc module) and the infrastructure spec for #666 (tracemalloc). Pre-clarification Q1 is entirely about gc.rs: atomic RC + cycle-detecting GC, mark-sweep with safepoint protocol, threshold at 700 allocs, gc.collect() triggering full mark-sweep, gc.disable()/enable() toggling automatic trigger. runtime/gc.md directly specifies: R2 (Mark-Sweep Collection Algorithm — mark phase, trace phase, sweep phase, reset phase), R3 (Cycle Detection and Reclamation), R4 (__del__ Finalizer Invocation with resurrection semantics), R5 (Configurable Collection Thresholds — threshold_allocs matching Q1's '700 allocs', manual gc.collect() trigger). Pre-clarification Q3 (tracemalloc) states gc.rs tracks container objects in a global set — gc.md R1 (Track Container Objects) defines this global container list backing tracemalloc get_traced_memory() coarse stats. The spec exists at cclab/specs/cclab-mamba/runtime/gc.md.
issues.recommendation: Add runtime/gc.md at HIGH relevance. Key requirements: R2 (Mark-Sweep Algorithm — gc.collect() triggers full cycle per Q1), R3 (Cycle Detection — foundation for GC correctness), R4 (__del__ Finalizer — gc module must expose finalizer behavior), R5 (Configurable Thresholds — gc.get_threshold()/gc.set_threshold() backed by threshold_allocs), R1 (Container Tracking — foundation for tracemalloc get_traced_memory() coarse stats per Q3).

issues.description: runtime/module.md is absent. This is the primary spec for #655 (importlib). Pre-clarification Q2 is entirely about module.rs: thread-local MODULES HashMap, MbModule with name/file/attrs/is_package, mb_import() with circular import protection (sentinel pre-caching), mb_import_from() for selective imports, find_module() for search path. runtime/module.md directly specifies: R1 (Module path resolution — check current dir, sys.path, dotted names), R2 (Module caching via sys.modules — insert before executing body; importlib.import_module() delegates here per Q2), R3 (Circular import handling — sentinel pre-caching matches Q2 exactly), R4 (import/from-import syntax — mb_import_from() backing importlib selective import). The spec exists at cclab/specs/cclab-mamba/runtime/module.md.
issues.recommendation: Add runtime/module.md at HIGH relevance. Key requirements: R2 (Module caching — importlib.import_module() delegates to mb_import() per Q2), R3 (Circular import handling — sentinel pre-caching matches Q2 answer), R1 (Module path resolution — find_module() backing importlib resources submodule), R4 (import/from-import — mb_import_from() backing importlib selective import).

issues.description: runtime/builtins.md is absent. This is the primary spec for #654 (types module). Pre-clarification Q4 explicitly states: type() builtin works at builtins.rs:475 returning Instance with __name__ field; FunctionType/GeneratorType/ModuleType etc. are NOT exposed as runtime type objects and need to be synthesized as Mamba class objects. runtime/builtins.md R5 (Enhanced P1 Builtins) covers the 20 additional builtin functions that form the type() infrastructure. R6 (Symbol Registration) defines how new type-object constants must be registered as mb_builtin_* symbols. R3 (Type Checking Builtins — isinstance, issubclass) is directly relevant because types.FunctionType enables isinstance(fn, types.FunctionType) checks. The spec exists at cclab/specs/cclab-mamba/runtime/builtins.md.
issues.recommendation: Add runtime/builtins.md at HIGH relevance. Key requirements: R5 (Enhanced P1 Builtins — type() at builtins.rs:475 per Q4, entry point for type-object synthesis), R6 (Symbol Registration — new FunctionType/GeneratorType/ModuleType constants need mb_* symbols registered), R3 (Type Checking Builtins — isinstance/issubclass depend on type objects exposed by types module).

issues.description: stdlib/native-implementations.md is absent. This is the implementation-pattern spec for ALL 7 in-scope issues. Every issue (#652 atexit, #653 gc, #654 types, #655 importlib, #656 codecs, #657 errno, #666 tracemalloc) requires creating a new native stdlib module file under runtime/stdlib/*.rs and registering it. stdlib/native-implementations.md defines: R2 (Module Rewrite — new *_mod.rs files follow this pattern), R3 (Refined Symbol Registration — all new module symbols must be registered via register_symbols() in symbols.rs), R4 (Error Mapping — Rust errors must map to MbException with appropriate types). The registration flow sequenceDiagram (runtime → stdlib_mod → module_registry → symbols_rs → JIT module) applies to every new module. The error mapping flowchart is required for #656 (codecs encoding errors), #657 (errno libc errors), and #655 (importlib ModuleNotFoundError). The spec exists at cclab/specs/cclab-mamba/stdlib/native-implementations.md.
issues.recommendation: Add stdlib/native-implementations.md at HIGH relevance. Key requirements: R2 (Module Rewrite — atexit_mod.rs, gc_mod.rs, types_mod.rs, importlib_mod.rs, codecs_mod.rs, errno_mod.rs, tracemalloc_mod.rs must all follow this pattern), R3 (Symbol Registration — every new module adds mb_* symbols to symbols.rs), R4 (Error Mapping — codec errors, errno libc errors, and ModuleNotFoundError must map to MbException).

issues.description: runtime/symbols.md is absent. Every new native stdlib module adds mb_* symbol registrations to symbols.rs. symbols.md R3 (register_symbols() Function) defines the exact registration protocol — cast function pointer to *const u8, register (name, address) pair with JIT module. R2 (MirExtern Declarations) defines the param_count/has_return fields required for each new symbol. R4 (Naming Convention) specifies the mb_<category>_<operation> pattern that new gc module symbols (mb_gc_collect, mb_gc_disable, mb_gc_enable) and stdlib module symbols must follow. This is a direct implementation requirement for all 7 issues. The spec exists at cclab/specs/cclab-mamba/runtime/symbols.md.
issues.recommendation: Add runtime/symbols.md at MEDIUM relevance. Key requirements: R3 (register_symbols() — all new module symbols must be registered before JIT-compiled code can call them), R2 (MirExtern Declarations — each new mb_gc_*/mb_importlib_*/mb_tracemalloc_* symbol needs a MirExtern entry), R4 (Naming Convention — mb_gc_collect, mb_gc_disable, mb_gc_enable, mb_tracemalloc_start must follow mb_<category>_<operation> pattern).

issues.description: pattern-matching.md is included at LOW relevance but has no connection to this group. Its eight requirements cover PEP 634 match/case: R1 (parser pattern types), R2 (AS patterns), R3 (mapping **rest), R4 (case-branch type narrowing), R5 (HIR pattern nodes), R6 (AST→HIR lowering), R7 (nested pattern MIR), R8 (Cranelift codegen for dispatch). None of these address atexit hooks, GC collect/disable/enable, type object synthesis, importlib import machinery, codec registry, errno constants, or tracemalloc snapshots. The reference context's justification cites ast and inspect modules — both of which are in the stdlib-introspection group, not stdlib-system.
issues.recommendation: Remove pattern-matching.md from this group's reference context entirely. Including it at any relevance level implies a connection that the pre-clarifications do not support and would mislead an implementer.

issues.description: No stdlib specs exist for atexit, codecs, or errno modules. stdlib/atexit.md, stdlib/codecs.md, and stdlib/errno.md are absent from the spec tree. These three issues (#652, #656, #657) require new specs to be created as part of this change before implementation can begin. The reference context cannot include them since they do not yet exist, but the gap means the change spec must explicitly schedule their creation.
issues.recommendation: Note in the change spec that cclab/specs/cclab-mamba/stdlib/atexit.md, codecs.md, and errno.md are new spec deliverables. atexit.md must specify: register()/unregister() API, call order (LIFO), exception suppression, integration with Rust atexit/at_exit hooks. codecs.md must specify: encode()/decode() dispatch, lookup() returning CodecInfo, register() for custom codecs, UTF-8/ASCII/Latin-1 built-in codec implementations. errno.md must specify: platform errno constant table (ENOENT, EACCES, EEXIST, ...), libc crate binding, errorcode reverse-lookup dict.
